findings.evidence: Lines 71-84 contain the hand induction and four `show` rewrites, including `rw [show coeff ((m x : ℤ) • ofPoint x) x = (m x : ℤ) by simp [coeff, hpoint]]` and `rw [show coeff (ofFinsetWithMultiplicity s m) x = ... by simpa [coeff] using hsx]`.
findings.fix: Replace the induction with a direct coefficient-evaluation proof using `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity`, `coeff`, `Finsupp.smul_apply`/sum application, and `Finset.sum_eq_single` or `simp`; if a `show` cast remains necessary, add a comment explaining the obstruction.
findings.issue: `coeff_ofFinsetWithMultiplicity` uses several undocumented `show ... by simp [coeff, ...]` rewrites inside a manual induction, obscuring why coefficient evaluation of the finite sum works and making the proof sensitive to wrapper/defeq changes.

summary: The new API mostly uses short automation, but the main coefficient lemma is a brittle hand proof relying on undocumented `show` rewrites and coefficient definitional unfolding.
